desc: Imports radiators from a JSON file (positions) and an Excel schedule (family/type by length).
summary: Imports radiators from a JSON file (positions) and an Excel schedule (family/type by length). Each radiator block in the JSON is matched to a schedule row via the REFERENCE attribute. Uses JsonModelLoader.Load to parse the JSON model. Uses GetRadiatorsFromScheduleCatalogue.LoadFromExcel to read the Excel schedule. Uses GetCurrentViewLevel.Get and LevelResolver.GetNearestLevel for level resolution. Uses UnitsHelper.RelativeMmToFeet_XY_UseBaseZ for coordinate conversion. Called by the ribbon or AI chat via MEPBridge.Revit.AIChat.Services.Execution.CommandExecutor. Related: ImportCommand for full MEP import.
usecase: User wants to place radiators in Revit from a design tool export. User has a JSON with radiator positions and an Excel schedule with sizing data. User needs to match radiator types to positions via reference attributes.
notfor: Importing full MEP layouts with pipes, ducts, trays (use ImportCommand). Manually placing individual radiators (use Revit's family placement). Editing existing radiator parameters (use parameter editing commands).
precondition: Active document must be open. A JSON file with radiator blocks and an Excel schedule with Length/Family/Type columns must be available. At least one level must exist.
postcondition: Radiator family instances are placed at JSON coordinates with types from the Excel schedule. Match results are exported to a rad-matches.json file alongside the source JSON. Schedule input is exported to rad-schedule-input.json for comparison.

config_inputs.name: jsonPath
config_inputs.description: Path to JSON file with radiator positions

config_inputs.name: schedulePath
config_inputs.description: Path to Excel schedule with Length/Family/Type columns

preprompts: Importing radiators from JSON and schedule...
Placing radiators from your design data...
thinkingsteps: Matching blocks to schedule rows by reference...
